name: cargo_runner
description: Run Cargo commands (build, test, check, clippy, run) with automatic output limiting and working directory management. Supports building specific packages, test filtering, error-only output mode, and result summarization. The --stash flag automatically stashes working changes before running and pops them after, enabling the common save-test-restore workflow in one call.
when_to_use: Use when working on Rust projects with Cargo. Replaces 'cd <project> && cargo build 2>&1 | tail -30' sequences. Supports: build (compile with optional package filter), test (run tests with optional name filter), check (fast compilation check), clippy (linting), and run (execute binary). Use --tail to show only the last N lines of output (prevents token overflow from long compile outputs). Use --errors-only to show only error lines (useful for quick compilation checks after edits, replaces 'cargo build | grep -E "^error"'). Use --package to build/test a specific workspace member. Use --stash to stash changes, run the command on the clean working tree, then restore changes -- replaces the 3-step stash/build/pop workflow with a single call.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Rust/Cargo project (e.g., /app)

parameters.name: --package/-p
parameters.description: Specific workspace package to build/test (e.g., 'boa_engine'). Default: entire workspace.

parameters.name: --tail/-t
parameters.description: Show only the last N lines of output (default: 30 for build/check/clippy, 0/unlimited for test).

parameters.name: --errors-only
parameters.description: Show only error lines (filters output to lines matching 'error' patterns). Useful for quick compilation error checks after edits, replaces 'cargo build 2>&1 | grep -E "^error"'.

parameters.name: --filter/-f
parameters.description: For 'test' action: only run tests matching this name filter (passed to cargo test).

parameters.name: --manifest-path
parameters.description: Path to a specific Cargo.toml (overrides project_root).

parameters.name: --args
parameters.description: Extra arguments to pass through to cargo (e.g., '-- --nocapture' for test).

parameters.name: --stash
parameters.description: Stash uncommitted changes, run the cargo command on the clean working tree, then pop the stash. Useful for verifying the original code compiles before applying edits.

examples.expected: Builds the boa_engine package and shows last 30 lines of output.
Builds boa_engine and shows only error lines. Useful after making edits to check for compilation errors without seeing warnings.
Runs tests matching 'cancellation' in the boa_engine package.
Runs clippy linter on boa_engine package.
Runs all tests with all features enabled and stdout not captured.
Stashes current changes, runs cargo check on clean code, then restores stashed changes.
Stashes changes, builds boa_engine, then restores changes.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate steps (cd to project, construct cargo command, pipe through tail or grep for output limiting) with a single script call. Eliminates the common error of forgetting --package or 2>&1 redirection. The --errors-only flag replaces the 'cargo build 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5' pattern with a single flag, saving an additional pipe/grep step when you only need error info. The --stash flag replaces the 3-step 'stash + cargo + pop' workflow with a single call, saving 2 tool calls each time you need to verify a clean build. The tail-based output limiting prevents token overflow from long compiler outputs (Rust compile errors can be hundreds of lines). The built-in test name filter avoids needing separate grep commands to find relevant test output.
